concept_options.title: He Chose Her First
concept_options.hook: He saved his mistress first, then left his Luna bleeding in the snow.
concept_options.visual_approach: Fast comic-panel escalation from blizzard abandonment to shelter replacement, then home rejection, left-hand severance, and fireplace control. Prioritize five hero frames with slow push-ins, hard shadow cuts, phrase captions, and 4-5 speech bubbles.
concept_options.target_audience: Western short-form paranormal romance and rejected-mate recap viewers who want immediate betrayal and a clean final escape hook.
key_points: Lucien publicly saves Seraphina while Evelyn is badly injured.
Seraphina occupies Evelyn's Luna symbolism before Evelyn even recovers.
Caspian and Lucien each make Evelyn understand she is treated as a tool, not family.
Evelyn signs the severance, but Lucien burns it, setting up a harder escape.
concept_options.core_message: This is the night Evelyn stops waiting to be chosen and starts planning to leave.
concept_options.cta: Follow for the next arc: what happens when her only safe exit is no longer a peaceful one.
concept_options.why_this_works: It opens with the strongest source-supported betrayal and preserves the arc landing: psychological severance before future revenge reveals.

concept_options.title: The Luna Blanket Was On Another Woman
concept_options.hook: She survived the mountain, only to find another woman wearing her title.
concept_options.visual_approach: Center the black-and-gold Luna blanket, wolf charm, Luna ring, and burned letter as repeated identity symbols. The edit uses tighter close-ups and more object inserts to make replacement feel ceremonial.
concept_options.target_audience: Viewers drawn to public humiliation, identity theft, and symbolic revenge setups.
key_points: Seraphina does not just receive care; she publicly takes Evelyn's visual position.
Caspian repeats that replacement pattern inside the Manor.
Evelyn rejects the symbols that kept her tied to Blackthorn Pack.
concept_options.core_message: Every symbol of Evelyn's old life is stolen, returned, or burned in one night.
concept_options.cta: Watch next to see what she does when the Pack refuses to release her.
concept_options.why_this_works: It gives the visual team strong recurring props and keeps the full arc coherent without spoiling later plot mechanics.

concept_options.title: She Signed With Her Left Hand
concept_options.hook: They crushed her right hand, so she used the left one to end everything.
concept_options.visual_approach: Begin with the left-hand signing as a cold open, then flash back through the three betrayals that forced it. This version is more psychological and less chronological, with the ring/fireplace as the main final visual rhyme.
concept_options.target_audience: Viewers who prefer quiet female-lead resolve and cold revenge setup over pure suffering montage.
key_points: Evelyn's injury becomes a visible measure of what the Pack took from her.
Her calm severance is stronger than an outburst.
Lucien's burned-letter refusal proves she will need a different kind of escape.
concept_options.core_message: Evelyn's first act of freedom is quiet, deliberate, and immediately challenged.
concept_options.cta: The next arc begins when peaceful severance is no longer enough.
concept_options.why_this_works: It highlights Evelyn's agency, but it sacrifices some of the raw opening shock compared with the betrayal-first structure.

selected_concept.rationale: Recommended pending user approval: it has the clearest short-form hook, covers every required arc node in order, and delays future reveals correctly.
modifications: Use the provided character reference image for Evelyn, Lucien, and Seraphina continuity.
Default to no music; use environmental sound only if added later.

stages.approach: Completed from local arc design, chapter breakdown, final chapter text, bible, stage plan, character reference, and visual style.
stages.fallback_if_unavailable: Blocked if source files are missing.

stages.approach: Write a 90-second English recap script that compresses six arc nodes into fast emotional escalation, with 3-6 speech bubbles and phrase captions.
stages.fallback_if_unavailable: Revise manually; no generation tool required.

stages.approach: Plan 8-10 vertical comic scenes with safe zones, overlay bubbles, image requirements, and no baked-in image text.
stages.fallback_if_unavailable: Reduce scene count to maintain asset feasibility.

tools.why_this_provider: Only configured TTS provider; free local path.

tools.role: Import generated or prepared comic stills into the project asset manifest
tools.why_this_provider: No API key required and compatible with user-supplied character reference workflow.

tools.role: Optional still-image generation path if local diffusion is used
tools.why_this_provider: Configured image capability exists, but exact generation route should be confirmed before asset stage.
Not configured; default proposal avoids added music.
Free/no-key, but external scraping and the visual style recommends no added music.

stages.approach: Use character reference and visual style to create/import comic stills, generate local TTS narration, and skip music unless approved.
stages.fallback_if_unavailable: If image generation/import is blocked, pause with missing-assets list before compose.

stages.approach: Create edit decisions with phrase captions, captionHighlightMode none, 4-5 speech bubbles, and Remotion motion instructions.
stages.fallback_if_unavailable: Simplify overlay count or merge scenes, but do not change runtime without approval.

tools.role: Render 9:16 final video through approved runtime
tools.why_this_provider: Required compose tool with Remotion render engine available.

tools.role: Mix narration and any approved environmental audio

tools.why_this_provider: Unavailable on this machine; listed for runtime audit.

stages.approach: Render with Remotion if approved; perform ffprobe and frame-sampling checks after render.
stages.fallback_if_unavailable: If Remotion becomes unavailable after approval, stop and request a new runtime decision.

stages.approach: Prepare title, description, thumbnail direction, and platform metadata after final render.
stages.fallback_if_unavailable: No publish action without final human approval.

quality_tradeoffs.quality_impact: Remotion is available and fits speech bubbles, phrase captions, still-panel motion, and audio timing; HyperFrames could offer GSAP typography but is unavailable and less aligned with this comic-panel workflow.

quality_tradeoffs.tradeoff: Piper local TTS versus paid cloud voices
quality_tradeoffs.recommendation: Use Piper unless you want to configure a premium voice provider.
quality_tradeoffs.quality_impact: Piper is free and available but less polished than ElevenLabs/OpenAI/Doubao; paid voices would improve performance and emotion.

quality_tradeoffs.tradeoff: No music versus royalty-free music search
quality_tradeoffs.recommendation: No music or minimal environmental audio.
quality_tradeoffs.quality_impact: Matches the style guide and avoids melodrama; music could add energy but risks fighting the cold noir tone.

quality_tradeoffs.tradeoff: Still-led comic recap versus true AI video generation
quality_tradeoffs.quality_impact: Video generation providers are not configured, and this pipeline is designed for animated comic panels rather than fully generated motion clips.

alternative_paths.description: Zero-key local path: local Piper TTS, generated/imported comic stills, Remotion composition, no added music.
alternative_paths.what_changes: Best available now; voice quality and image generation options are limited by local setup.

alternative_paths.description: Premium voice upgrade: configure ElevenLabs, OpenAI, Google, or Doubao TTS before assets.
alternative_paths.what_changes: Improves narration performance while keeping the same visual plan.

alternative_paths.description: Premium media upgrade: configure FAL/OpenAI/Google/xAI image or video providers.
alternative_paths.what_changes: Adds broader image style control or true motion clips, but requires API keys and a revised asset plan.

music_source.mood_direction: No added music by default; optional restrained environmental sounds such as wind, helicopter distance, footsteps, study fire, and paper burn.

voice_selection.rationale: Only configured TTS provider in preflight; free and available for English narration.

image.explanation: Best no-key path for importing generated/reference-guided stills after approval.
Configured image capability, but actual output consistency needs asset-stage verification.

tts.explanation: Available free local narration; lower emotional nuance than premium providers.
Strong fit and quality but unavailable until ELEVENLABS_API_KEY is configured.

music.explanation: Default visual style explicitly recommends no added music.
Available and free, but less aligned with the no-music policy.

video.explanation: Available render engine for animated still panels, captions, and bubbles.
Potentially useful for kinetic typography but unavailable now.

line_items.operation: Generate approximately 90 seconds of English narration
Create or import approximately 8-10 comic stills after approval

line_items.notes: Local render; will require sandbox escalation for render/browser/encoder access.

line_items.operation: Mix narration with optional environmental audio

savings_options: Already on zero-key path; main savings choice is to keep no music and avoid paid video generation.

approval.user_notes: User approved the recommended path: c1 concept, Remotion runtime, Piper local TTS, and no added music.

preflight_notes: Required TTS and compose tools are available.

decisions.subject: Production pipeline

options_considered.label: novel-comic-recap-arc
options_considered.reason: User explicitly requested this pipeline and supplied novel project path plus arc id.

options_considered.label: generic animation pipeline
options_considered.reason: Could render animation, but lacks novel arc intake and spoiler-boundary contract.

decisions.reason: Matches user request and source structure exactly.

decisions.subject: Concept direction

options_considered.label: He Chose Her First
options_considered.reason: Strongest short-form hook and complete node coverage.

options_considered.label: The Luna Blanket Was On Another Woman
options_considered.reason: Excellent symbolic treatment but less immediate than the survival betrayal hook.

options_considered.label: She Signed With Her Left Hand
options_considered.reason: Strong agency angle but softens the opening shock.

decisions.reason: Recommended because it opens with the source-supported highest-intensity betrayal while preserving the severance landing.

decisions.subject: Creative renderer family

options_considered.label: Animation-first comic recap
options_considered.reason: Fits still comic panels, captions, bubbles, and controlled vertical motion.

options_considered.label: Cinematic trailer
options_considered.reason: Would imply true motion clips; video generation providers are unavailable and the requested format is recap drama.
options_considered.rejected_because: Wrong delivery character for available tools.

decisions.reason: Best match for one-arc comic recap with still-led images and motion overlays.

decisions.subject: Render runtime

options_considered.label: Remotion
options_considered.reason: Available; best fit for animated still panels, speech bubbles, phrase captions, and audio timing.

options_considered.label: HyperFrames
options_considered.reason: Could support HTML/GSAP kinetic typography, but it is unavailable on this machine.
options_considered.rejected_because: runtime not available on this machine

options_considered.label: FFmpeg-only
options_considered.reason: Available for simple concat/trim but weaker for controlled captions and comic bubbles.

decisions.reason: Recommended because Remotion is available and aligned with the pipeline default for comic-panel motion and overlay control.

decisions.subject: Narration voice provider

options_considered.label: Piper local TTS
options_considered.reason: Available and free, suitable for zero-key draft/presentable path.

options_considered.label: ElevenLabs TTS
options_considered.reason: Higher emotional quality but unavailable until API key setup.

options_considered.label: OpenAI TTS
options_considered.reason: Potential high quality but unavailable until API key setup.

decisions.reason: Only configured provider; keeps production zero-key unless user chooses an upgrade.

decisions.subject: Music and sound policy

options_considered.label: No added music
options_considered.reason: Matches visual style SOUND_POLICY and avoids melodrama.

options_considered.label: Pixabay Music
options_considered.reason: Available free/no-key search, but external scraping and less aligned with the cold style.

options_considered.label: ElevenLabs music generation
options_considered.reason: Could generate music but unavailable until API key setup.

decisions.reason: Style policy defaults to no added music; environmental audio can be considered later if needed.

decisions.subject: Motion requirement classification

options_considered.label: Still-led animated comic recap
options_considered.reason: Matches pipeline and available tools; motion comes from panel animation and overlays.

options_considered.label: True generated motion clips
options_considered.reason: No configured video generation providers and not necessary for this recap format.

decisions.reason: The delivery promise can be presentable with animated still panels, so true motion is not a hard requirement.

findings.title: Piper voice quality tradeoff
findings.description: Local Piper TTS is free and available but may be less emotionally polished than paid voices.
findings.action: Accepted by approval; continue with Piper unless user later requests a voice upgrade.

findings.title: HyperFrames unavailable
findings.description: Runtime option was considered and rejected because it is unavailable on this machine.
findings.action: Accepted by approval; continue with Remotion.
